title: Add flow-field ribbon shader variant
overview: Create a new flow-field ribbon shader variant that displaces points along curl noise flow-field lines with elongated velocity-aligned sprites. Bass modulates flow speed, treble modulates turbulence. The system integrates as a new mode in the existing ModeManager rotation alongside the six existing geometry systems.

steps.title: Create flowRibbon vertex shader (flowRibbon.vert.glsl)
steps.description: Write a new vertex shader that displaces points along curl3 flow-field lines. Key differences from existing warp shaders: (1) compute a velocity vector from curl3 at each point position, (2) advect the point position along that velocity scaled by uBassEnergy (flow speed), (3) use a second curl3 sample at higher frequency for turbulence scaled by uTrebleEnergy, (4) elongate gl_PointSize along the velocity direction using the velocity magnitude. The shader uses the same uniform set as existing systems (COMMON_UNIFORMS) plus a new uFlowScale uniform for advection strength. Must declare all attributes matching RIBBONFIELD_ATTRIBUTES plus optional 'size'.

details.attributes: position(3), color(3), aHueOffset(1), aRandom(3), size(1, optional)
details.key_algorithm: vec3 vel = curl3(pos * uNoiseFrequency * 0.5 + vec3(uTime * 0.00005 * uCadence), uNoiseOctaves); float speed = length(vel); vel *= uBassEnergy * uFlowScale; pos += vel * 0.4; vec3 turb = curl3(pos * uNoiseFrequency * 1.5 + vec3(uTime * 0.0001), max(1, uNoiseOctaves - 1)); pos += turb * uTrebleEnergy * 0.15; gl_PointSize scaled by (1.0 + speed * 2.0) for elongation effect
details.distinct_character: Smooth, streaming tendril motion vs the sculptural displacement of pointWarp or the sinusoidal sway of ribbonWarp. Points flow like smoke or aurora.

steps.title: Create flowRibbon fragment shader (flowRibbon.frag.glsl)
steps.description: Write a fragment shader that renders elongated soft sprites. Use gl_PointCoord to create an elliptical falloff (stretched along the velocity axis passed via vElongation varying). Apply the same atmospheric fog and depth desaturation as ribbonWarp.frag.glsl, with additive blending support. The elongation creates the tendril/streak appearance without needing line geometry.

details.key_technique: Stretch gl_PointCoord circle into ellipse: vec2 uv = gl_PointCoord - 0.5; uv.x *= mix(1.0, 0.4, vElongation); float d = length(uv); alpha = smoothstep(0.5, 0.35, d)
details.fog_model: Same depth fog as ribbonWarp.frag.glsl (uFogNear/uFogFar uniforms)

steps.title: Register flowRibbon attributes in shaderRegistry.ts
steps.description: Add FLOWRIBBON_ATTRIBUTES and OPTIONAL_FLOWRIBBON_ATTRIBUTES exports to shaderRegistry.ts. These match the RIBBONFIELD_ATTRIBUTES (position, color, aHueOffset, aRandom) with optional size attribute, same as existing ribbon system.

steps.title: Create flowRibbonField geometry system (src/visual/systems/flowRibbonField.ts)
steps.description: Implement the FlowRibbonField system following the same factory pattern as ribbonField.ts. Create initial point positions using seeded streamlines (not helical bands like ribbonField — instead, seed points along random positions and pre-advect them a few steps along curl3 to form natural streamline clusters). This gives the initial geometry an organic, flowing distribution. Wire up all COMMON_UNIFORMS plus uFlowScale. Implement init(), draw(), setOpacity(), cleanup() per GeometrySystem interface.

details.config_interface: FlowRibbonFieldConfig { maxPoints?, enableSparkle?, noiseOctaves?, enablePointerRepulsion?, enableSlowModulation? }
details.initial_geometry: Seed N source points randomly in a sphere, then for each source generate a short streamline (5-8 trail points) by iteratively advecting along curl3. This creates natural tendril/filament clusters.
details.material_config: ShaderMaterial with AdditiveBlending, transparent, depthWrite:false, noise3d.glsl prepended to vertex shader

steps.title: Add quality profile field for flowRibbon
steps.description: Add maxFlowRibbonPoints to QualityProfile interface and set values per tier: low=200, medium=700, high=1600. This follows the same pattern as maxRibbonPoints.

steps.title: Register flowRibbonField as a new mode in main.ts
steps.description: Import createFlowRibbonField, instantiate it with quality-driven config, and add it to the ModeManager modes array as { name: 'flowribbon', factory: () => flowRibbon }. Also add it to the debug modes array for point count reporting.

details.insertion_point: After wireframe creation (line ~138), before createModeManager call
details.mode_position: Add after wirepolyhedra in the modes array

steps.title: Verify shader compilation and runtime
steps.description: Run npm run build to verify TypeScript compilation succeeds. Then run the dev server and verify: (1) no WebGL shader compile errors in console, (2) no VALIDATE_STATUS errors, (3) the flowRibbon mode renders visible geometry when it becomes active in the mode rotation, (4) bass energy visibly affects flow speed, (5) treble energy visibly affects turbulence/detail, (6) visual character is distinctly different from existing warp shaders (smoke/aurora/tendril vs sculptural/crystalline).

details.health_gate: Existing US-048/US-052 shader error capture and health gate will automatically validate the new shaders during startup

keyRisks.risk: Double curl3 evaluation per vertex (flow + turbulence) may be expensive on low-tier GPUs
keyRisks.mitigation: Low quality tier uses 1 noise octave and can skip the turbulence curl3 pass entirely via a conditional based on uNoiseOctaves

keyRisks.risk: Elongated point sprites via gl_PointSize have a hardware max (gl_PointSizeRange), large sizes may clip
keyRisks.mitigation: Clamp gl_PointSize to a reasonable max (64.0) and rely more on the elliptical fragment shader falloff for the elongation visual

keyRisks.risk: Streamline-based initial geometry may produce clumped or sparse distributions for certain seeds
keyRisks.mitigation: Use adaptive source point count and cap trail length; add a minimum spacing check during streamline generation

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.A new vertex shader displaces points alo: Step 1 — flowRibbon.vert.glsl uses curl3 for primary displacement
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Points are rendered as elongated sprites: Steps 1-2 — velocity magnitude passed as vElongation varying, fragment shader stretches gl_PointCoord into ellipse
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Flow speed is modulated by bass energy: Step 1 — curl3 velocity vector scaled by uBassEnergy
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Flow turbulence is modulated by treble e: Step 1 — secondary high-frequency curl3 displacement scaled by uTrebleEnergy
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL er: Step 7 — verified via build + existing health gate (US-048/US-052)
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Can be assigned to particleField or poin: Steps 3-4 — uses same attribute layout as ribbonField, registered in shaderRegistry, compatible geometry system interface
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Visual character is distinctly different: Steps 1-2 — streaming/advection motion vs sculptural displacement; elongated sprites vs circular points; smoke/aurora aesthetic vs geometric deformation
